description: A well-judged, well-structured wine that balances fresh, crisp and light fruit against flavors of peaches, almonds and cinnamon. It will be better in two or three years' time.
title: Volpe Pasini 2002 Zuc de Volpe Pinot Grigio (Colli Orientali del Friuli)

description: Anise and mineral add character to the aggressive, starchy nose. Flavors of ripe apple and mustard create a heavier-than-usual palate, although there's ample acidity to keep it more or less balanced. The finish is dry, with just a touch of citrus flavor. And at the very end it turns thin and rough.
title: Guelbenzu 2002 Jardin Chardonnay (Colchagua Valley)

description: With vineyards in the southern vineyards of the Aube, Vincent Couche makes a rich style of Champagne. This 2002 vintage, now maturing well, is ripe—almost opulent—in character. Its peach and apricot flavors shine at the fore, extended on the palate with a brisk, nervy texture.
title: Vincent Couche 2002 Millésime Brut  (Champagne)

description: This has a touch of toast and a rich character. A creamed pear note mingles with lime-driven acidity and a delicious, warm tropical-fruit aftertaste.
title: Charles Ellner 2002 Séduction Brut  (Champagne)

description: Maturing well, this is a rich Champagne. Its fruit flavors have gained a golden glow of toast and almond flavors, while retaining a taut, textured acidity. Ready to drink now.
title: Veuve Doussot 2002 Cuvée Guy Joly Mémory Brut  (Champagne)

description: The mixed plum fruit is unremarkable but fine. In terms of texture, there's not much, until some powdery-woody tannins pop up on the back end.
title: Riverina Estates 2002 Kanga's Leap Cabernet Sauvignon (South Eastern Australia)

description: This sparkling wine has a fragrance of honey, almond and banana peel. The palate is a bit evolved, but shows ripe yellow apple, white chocolate, flint and pastry notes. It has an elegant perlage and closes on an almond note.
title: Lorenz Martini 2002 Comitissa Glod Gran Riserva Sparkling (Alto Adige)

description: This vibrant and sophisticated Cuvée shows aromas of sourdough, yellow fruit, subtle notes of exotic spice and dried apricot. The mouthfeel is dense and creamy and the wine boasts impressive persistency on the finish.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2002 Cuvée Annamaria Clementi Sparkling (Franciacorta)

description: What stands out here is the wine's complexity. Dried apricots, honey and almond paste are framed by a tiny hint of mint or chopped basil. The wine is very sweet in the mouth but it is also balanced out by a blast of fresh acidity on the close. A blend of Garganega, Sauvignon, Chardonnay and Cortese.
title: Monte del Frá 2002 Monastero Passito White (Veneto)

description: A full, rich, concentrated wine, with fat, ripe fruit and a touch of acidity. Big and powerful, it has a fresh, clean aftertaste.
title: Cantina Tramin 2002 Pinot Bianco (Alto Adige)

description: From what is claimed to be the highest vineyard in Europe, at 3,000 feet, comes this surprisingly ripe, full-flavored Müller-Thurgau, with soft acidity and a fine, crisp, green finish.
title: Tiefenbrunner 2002 Feldmarschall von Fenner Müller-Thurgau (Alto Adige)

description: A soft, rounded wine with hints of oak from the one-third barrel aging. Concentrated and firm, it is already well in balance, with soft, creamy flavors.
title: Elena Walch 2002 Kastelatz Pinot Bianco (Alto Adige)

description: Nice floral, pear and vanilla notes waft from the nose. Its body is on the soft side, but the sweetish, white stone fruit-and-citrus profile (and its bargain price) are surefire crowd pleasers.
title: Black Swan 2002 Chardonnay (South Eastern Australia)

description: This is a beautifully balanced, elegant and mature sparkler. There is fruit—ripe strawberry—but the main character is its fine balance and richness. It can age, though it is at its best now.
title: Billecart-Salmon 2002 Cuvée Elisabeth Salmon Brut Rosé  (Champagne)

description: Buttered baguette, apricot, chestnut honey and yellow rose describe the fragrant nose of this luminous sparkling wine from northern Italy. It boasts well-integrated acidity and lively freshness on the close.
title: Ca' del Bosco 2002 Dosage Zero Sparkling (Franciacorta)

description: A fresh, ripe style of wine, with spice and roundness. It has crisp citrus flavors to give it a lively aftertaste.
title: Borgo Conventi 2002 I Fiori del Borgo Pinot Grigio (Collio)

description: Ysios is a ripe, smooth wine with polished aromas of black fruit, charred meat and vanilla. The palate has black cherry, plum, bacon and chocolate flavors, and the finish is smoky. Throughout there's a nice grilled character to the wine, which isn't very complicated and isn't likely to improve much with age. Drink now through 2010.
title: Ysios 2002 Reserva  (Rioja)

description: On the nose, it's floral and sweet, with a sticky quality to the fruit. In the mouth, it's like lemon-lime soda, with some bitter green melon thrown in. And the feel is thin and acidic. A tangy, lean rendition of this potentially interesting variety.
title: Aresti 2002 Montemar Gewürztraminer (Curicó Valley)

description: Rather heavy in texture, with the weight of a Rhone wine, and thick, fruity flavors of plums, heirloom tomatoes, cola, espresso and oak. Very dry, with smooth tannins. This is a big Pinot, clean and complex, although not particularly delicate.
title: Russian Hill 2002 Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: An excellent example of this local grape variety, this is an aromatic, full-bodied wine, with ripe fruit. Aromas of lavender are followed on the palate by flavors of almonds and fresh, green fruits.
title: Volpe Pasini 2002 Luc de Volpe Tocai Friulano Tocai (Colli Orientali del Friuli)

description: Full bodied and viscous on the palate, the wine is balanced by bright acidity that gives it good focus. Peach, grapefruit, apple and spice notes are at the core here, followed by a lemony fresh finish. Quite fruity in style and fairly sweet. Try it as an aperitif or with duck and sweet sauces.
title: Maddalena 2002 Riesling (Monterey)

description: This certainly has maturity, giving it toasty flavors and a touch of oxidation. But it balances that with ripe yellow fruit, ripe acidity and a firm texture. It's rich and complex, balancing ripe fruit and more a rounded finish. Don't age it, though—enjoy it with food now.
title: Pannier 2002 Egérie Extra Brut  (Champagne)

description: At 70% Garnacha and 30% Tempranillo, this hold-over from the sketchy 2002 harvest starts with an odd blend of clove and shaved wood notes, while the palate pushes basic cherry and tangy raspberry flavors.
title: Valle Rojo 2002 Crianza Grenache-Tempranillo Red (Cariñena)

description: The depth and dimension of this wine are extraordinary and so are its aromas of plum, fresh berry, exotic spice, resin, pine forest, vanilla, bramble and chopped herb. These many nuances blend together to form a complex, balanced whole backed by solid structure and good intensity of flavors. The wine undergoes long maceration on the grape skins and ages two years in oak barrel.
title: Bressan 2002 Pinot Nero (Venezia Giulia)

description: This recently released 2002 is still showing its youth, with a firm, mineral edge to it. But it does have the right refreshingly crisp character, along with intense fruits, and acidity that just powers right through. Wait 3–4 years.
designation: Fleuron Premier Cru Blanc de Blancs Brut
title: Pierre Gimonnet et Fils 2002 Fleuron Premier Cru Blanc de Blancs Brut Chardonnay (Champagne)

description: Fairly forward, with aromas of lemon curd and the insides of many oak barrels. In the mouth are the full allotment of pear, apple and lemon flavors, while the finish sits down and dries out rather nicely. Tasty and refreshing, with just enough heft.
title: Morandé 2002 Vitisterra Grand Reserve Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

description: Simple, open and ready to drink. The nose is floral, with hints of fresh pear. The flavor profile turns to citrus in the form of orange and pineapple, and then comes a full-force but fairly collected finish.   Best Buy.
title: La Palma 2002 Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (Cachapoal Valley)

description: The nose is light and attractive, with peach and floral accents. The mouth is perfectly smooth, with apple, melon and a hint of flint or steel. On the finish, some cinnamon spice adds character to the baked-apple flavors. With no flaws, this is the epitome of affordable, drinkable Chardonnay. Best Buy.
title: Montes 2002 Reserve - Barrel Fermented Chardonnay (Curicó Valley)

description: Crisp raspberries and red currants here, with a refreshing, lively acidity. If there is any intensity in this finely balanced wine, it is in the relationship between the fruit and the clean structure. But this is not for aging—drink now.
title: Henriot 2002 Rosé Millésimé  (Champagne)

description: Clean pear fruit on the nose and refreshing throughout. This wine works because it's straightforward and lightly oaked. The apple, melon and mineral flavors stand out, and the round body and solid finish carry it home.
title: Viña Casas del Bosque 2002 Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)
